content: Ari flagged AWS spend spike in standup—$14.2k last month, up 31% YoY. Devon said new indexing on prod is the culprit. Suggested we model 12-month forward before next board call.
summary: AWS spend spike discussed; 12mo forecasting recommended

content: Reserved instances for RDS + compute savings plan combo typically yields 35–42% discount vs on-demand. Worth modeling given current burn. Snowflake contract also up for renewal in Q3.
summary: RI + savings plan combo yields 35–42% savings vs on-demand

content: When AWS pricing review needed: (1) get 90-day spend export from DataDog, (2) ask Ari/Devon for 12mo usage forecast by service, (3) run AWS calculator against reserved + savings plan scenarios, (4) doc assumptions in Linear ticket for audit trail.
summary: AWS pricing review workflow: DataDog export → forecast → calculator → Linear doc

content: Ari sent forecast model Wed evening—RDS $8.2k/mo baseline, compute $6.8k/mo, other services $4.1k/mo. Assumes 2.3x scaling through Q4. Conservative but plausible given Acme/Initech ramp.
summary: Ari's 12mo AWS forecast: $19.1k/mo baseline, 2.3x growth assumed

content: 1-year commitment typically requires 30–45 day notice on AWS. 3-year RIs lock in deeper discount (~60%) but risky if we migrate workload. Savings plans more flexible than RIs—cover any compute instance family.
summary: 1yr AWS commitments need 30–45d notice; savings plans more flexible than RIs

content: Called Anya Thursday afternoon to review Ari's numbers. She eyeballed the $19.1k/mo baseline and said 'feels right given headcount/workload.' Green-lit me to cost-optimize. Asked for final rec by EOD Friday.
summary: Anya approved cost-optimization work; wants final rec by Fri

content: AWS RDS: reserved instances for 1yr on db.r6i.2xlarge ~45% cheaper than on-demand. Upfront $4.8k covers ~10mo cost; remaining 2mo on-demand fallback. Works if baseline stable.

Devon and I walked through the AWS calculator results Friday morning. He pushed back on 2.3x growth assumption—said indexing optimizations coming in April will flatten compute by 12%. Revised forecast down to 1.8x.
summary: Devon questioned growth forecast; revised compute down due to April optimizations

content: For Clude's current infra footprint, optimal strategy: $120k 1yr compute savings plan + $60k RDS RI commitment = $180k total. Saves ~$67k annually vs on-demand. Requires commitment decision by Mar 20.
summary: $180k AWS commitment (savings plan + RI) saves $67k/yr; deadline Mar 20

content: Before recommending AWS spend commitment to Sebastien: (1) document assumption set (growth rate, service mix, migration risk), (2) model breakeven scenario if workload drops, (3) list any contract clauses permitting early termination or downgrade.
summary: Pre-recommendation checklist: assumptions doc, breakeven model, termination clauses

content: Sent Sebastien the AWS spreadsheet Monday morning—$180k commitment, 38% savings vs on-demand, assumes revised 1.8x growth. Flagged Mar 20 deadline. He asked one question: 'Can we scale down if customer churn happens?' Honest answer: RI/savings plan lock in capacity.
summary: Sebastien questioned downside risk if churn; RI/savings plan lock in capacity

content: Clude's customer concentration: Acme 28% ARR, Initech 19%, Globex 14%, Wayne 9%. If Acme churns, workload drops ~$5.2k/mo. RIs non-returnable but can be resold on AWS Marketplace at 60–75% recovery.
summary: Key risk: Acme 28% ARR; RI resale recovery ~60–75% if churn occurs

content: I hate re-explaining context. Realized this week I should've documented Ari/Devon's growth assumptions in Linear upfront instead of relying on email. Cost me 3 clarification rounds with Sebastien. Next time: Linear ticket + Notion doc before any spreadsheet.
summary: Lesson: doc assumptions in Linear/Notion first, avoid re-explaining via email

content: Anya called Wednesday to confirm ARR forecast overlaps with AWS growth model. She pulled Q2 pipeline: Wayne Industries likely +$180k, Globex +$120k. Bumps baseline workload assumption higher. Told her I'd retro-run numbers if deal closure dates firm.
summary: Anya's Q2 pipeline (Wayne +$180k, Globex +$120k) may justify higher AWS commitment

content: AWS savings plan commitment requires 1yr upfront payment or monthly installments. We chose 1yr upfront for Clude's cash position—$120k hit to Q1 but no surprise monthly invoices. Easier for Anya's forecasting.
summary: 1yr upfront AWS payment ($120k) chosen for cash flow predictability

content: Final rec sent to Sebastien Friday: proceed with $180k 1yr commitment. Attached risk doc—churn downside, March 20 deadline hard stop, RI resale option. He approved verbally. Told Ari to file purchase order with AWS by EOD Mar 17.
summary: Sebastien approved $180k AWS commitment; PO deadline Mar 17
